seoTitle: How to Add Contacts in Brevo Easily (2026)
metaDescription: A quick guide to expanding your Brevo subscriber database by manually creating contact profiles with names, lists, and opt-in consent.
intro: Growing your Brevo database doesn't always mean a bulk spreadsheet import. This guide walks through the single-contact creation wizard, covering how to enter core details, organize new contacts into the right list, and confirm marketing consent before saving. It's a fast option for adding leads one at a time as they come in.

steps.heading: Navigate to your contact console
steps.body: Log into Brevo and click Contacts in the left sidebar to open your subscriber database.

steps.heading: Open the contact creation form
steps.body: Click Add a contact in the upper right corner of the database grid.

steps.heading: Enter core information
steps.body: Type the email address into the primary field, then add optional details like first name, last name, or a mobile number.

steps.heading: Assign a target list
steps.body: Use the dropdown menu to select an existing folder group or create a fresh list container to hold the new contact.

steps.heading: Confirm consent and save
steps.body: Toggle the compliance checkbox confirming you have consent, click Create contact, then use the search bar to verify it saved correctly.

faq.question: What information is required to add a contact manually?
faq.answer: At minimum you need an email address, though you can also add optional fields like name and phone number for a more complete profile.

faq.question: Can I organize new contacts into multiple lists?
faq.answer: Yes, you can check the boxes next to every subscriber list the contact should belong to during creation.

faq.question: Is manual entry better than a bulk import for one contact?
faq.answer: Yes, manual entry is faster and avoids the formatting risks that come with spreadsheet uploads when you only need to add one record.

faq.question: How do I confirm my new contact saved properly?
faq.answer: Search for the email address in the master search bar at the top of the contact console to verify it appears in your database.
